meta.description: Daily UAE/ADNOC-focused energy intelligence. Splits config/energy-sources.txt into feeds, fetches them, aggregates real articles, analyses with local Ollama, renders HTML and writes report + archive via fs. Cover image is an optional side branch (Image Provider Abstraction). Every external step has a failure path.

nodes.name: Fetch Energy Sources
nodes.notes: Runs once per feed URL. Non-RSS or unreachable sources are skipped, not fatal.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "{{ $env.OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL || 'llama3.1:8b' }}",
  "stream": false,
  "keep_alive": "10m",
  "options": { "temperature": 0.2, "num_predict": 7500, "num_ctx": 12288 },
  "messages": [
    {"role":"system","content":"You are a senior energy-sector intelligence analyst at a top consultancy, producing a weekly regional energy summary for executives covering Middle East national oil companies (NOCs) and supermajor international oil companies (IOCs). Use ONLY facts found in the provided digests; never invent companies, names, numbers, deals or quotes. Write in a precise, engaging, magazine-quality editorial voice suitable for a premium intelligence publication. Output a SINGLE valid JSON object and nothing else (no markdown, no preamble, no code fences). Keys: weekOf (string); intro (a rich editorial overview of 2-3 paragraphs, about 8-12 sentences total, summarising the week's key findings; separate paragraphs with a single newline character; ONE paragraph MUST specifically cover oil and gas price levels and notable price movements this week); weekOverWeek (a short string, 1-2 sentences, summarising the most important changes since the previous edition; empty string if no previous edition is provided); financial (an object with keys: summary (1-2 sentence overview of the oil and gas price environment this week), prices (array of up to 5 items, each with keys name, value, change and note; cover at least Brent crude, WTI, natural gas (Henry Hub) and an LNG benchmark such as TTF or JKM; populate value and change from the LIVE MARKET PRICES block provided below; if a listed benchmark is absent there, set value and change to 'Not specified'), outlook (2-3 sentences on near-term price direction and explicit predictions), adnocTrading (2-3 sentences focused specifically on ADNOC Trading and ADNOC Global Trading - activity, strategy, deals or positioning), adnocFinancials (2-3 sentences of key ADNOC financial analysis such as capex, dividends, valuations, listings/IPOs or earnings)); featuredIntro, nocIntro, iocIntro, moversIntro (each a 1-2 sentence summary paragraph introducing that section); noc (array; item keys company, development, theme, signals, risk, watch); ioc (same item shape); movers (array up to 8 with keys company, individual, previous, current, date, why); featured (array of the 3-5 most interesting and important stories this week as short magazine write-ups; each item has keys headline, story which is 3-4 engaging sentences, source which is the article URL or outlet name, and image which is the IMG= URL for that story from the digest if present (else an empty string); ensure the biggest ADNOC developments feature prominently); bonus (object with keys title and body of 3 short paragraphs). ALWAYS include a row for EVERY one of these companies, even if quiet: NOC rows must cover ADNOC, Saudi Aramco, QatarEnergy and Kuwait Petroleum (KPC); IOC rows must cover Shell, BP, Chevron, ExxonMobil and TotalEnergies. If a company has no material news in the digest, still include its row with development 'No material developments this week.', theme 'Quiet week', signals 'Not specified', risk 'Low - no notable activity.' and watch 'Not specified.'. The news digest is labelled by company in square brackets; use those labels to attribute developments correctly. risk must begin with Low, Medium or High then a short rationale. For movers, use the LEADERSHIP/BOARD CHANGES DIGEST and include every appointment, resignation, promotion or board change; when a mover's name, previous role, new role or date is not stated, set that field to 'Not specified'. If a PREVIOUS EDITION SUMMARY is provided, make the brief week-over-week aware: populate weekOverWeek with the key changes since then and reflect momentum versus last edition in signals where relevant. Keep every text field concise (each development, signals, story, why and bonus paragraph no more than 2-3 sentences) so the COMPLETE JSON is always returned without truncation. Do NOT wrap the output in markdown code fences; your response MUST begin with { and end with }. Output ONLY the JSON object."},
    {"role":"user","content": {{ JSON.stringify('NEWS DIGEST for ' + ($json.date||'') + ' (' + ($json.count||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.articles||'') + '

LEADERSHIP/BOARD CHANGES DIGEST (' + ($json.moversCount||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.moversDigest||'None retrieved.') + '

PREVIOUS EDITION SUMMARY:
' + ($json.previous||'No previous edition available.') + '

LIVE MARKET PRICES (use these EXACT figures for the financial.prices array, and reference the levels/moves in your outlook):
' + ($json.pricesDigest||'Not available this run.')) }} }
  ]
}

nodes.name: Generate Cover (Image Provider)
nodes.notes: Optional cover via Image Provider Abstraction. Dead-end side branch: failure (e.g. no API key) never affects the brief.

nodes.name: Write Report + Archive
nodes.notes: Writes via fs (NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN=fs) to avoid the Read/Write File node's binary requirement.

nodes.name: Email Brief (provider-abstracted)
nodes.notes: Email provider selected via EMAIL_PROVIDER; fails gracefully if SMTP not configured.

nodes.name: Reduced Coverage Notice
nodes.notes: Failure path: emit a reduced-coverage notice + log instead of producing nothing.

nodes.name: AI Analyst (Claude)

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "{{ $env.CLAUDE_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}",
  "max_tokens": 16000,
  "system": "You are a senior energy-sector intelligence analyst at a top consultancy, producing a weekly regional energy summary for executives covering Middle East national oil companies (NOCs) and supermajor international oil companies (IOCs). Use ONLY facts found in the provided digests; never invent companies, names, numbers, deals or quotes. Write in a precise, engaging, magazine-quality editorial voice suitable for a premium intelligence publication. Output a SINGLE valid JSON object and nothing else (no markdown, no preamble, no code fences). ALWAYS include a row for EVERY one of these companies, even if quiet: NOC rows must cover ADNOC, Saudi Aramco, QatarEnergy and Kuwait Petroleum (KPC); IOC rows must cover Shell, BP, Chevron, ExxonMobil and TotalEnergies. If a company has no material news in the digest, still include its row with development 'No material developments this week.', theme 'Quiet week', signals 'Not specified', risk 'Low - no notable activity.' and watch 'Not specified.'. The news digest is labelled by company in square brackets; use those labels to attribute developments correctly. risk must begin with Low, Medium or High then a short rationale. For movers, use the LEADERSHIP/BOARD CHANGES DIGEST and include every appointment, resignation, promotion or board change; when a mover's name, previous role, new role or date is not stated, set that field to 'Not specified'. If a PREVIOUS EDITION SUMMARY is provided, make the brief week-over-week aware: populate weekOverWeek with the key changes since then and reflect momentum versus last edition in signals where relevant. Keep every text field concise (each development, signals, story, why and bonus paragraph no more than 2-3 sentences) so the COMPLETE JSON is always returned without truncation. Do NOT wrap the output in markdown code fences; your response MUST begin with { and end with }. Output ONLY the JSON object.",
  "messages": [
    {"role":"user","content": {{ JSON.stringify('NEWS DIGEST for ' + ($json.date||'') + ' (' + ($json.count||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.articles||'') + '

LEADERSHIP/BOARD CHANGES DIGEST (' + ($json.moversCount||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.moversDigest||'None retrieved.') + '

PREVIOUS EDITION SUMMARY:
' + ($json.previous||'No previous edition available.') + '

LIVE MARKET PRICES (use these EXACT figures for the financial.prices array, and reference the levels/moves in your outlook):
' + ($json.pricesDigest||'Not available this run.')) }} }
  ]
}

nodes.notes: Anthropic Claude path (AI_PROVIDER=claude). Higher-quality analysis. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

nodes.name: Build Cover File
nodes.notes: Cover page is its own PDF: full-bleed, no header/footer, no page number.

nodes.name: Gotenberg Cover
nodes.notes: Cover HTML -> single full-bleed PDF page (margins 0, no header/footer).

nodes.name: Stash Cover
nodes.notes: Carry the cover PDF as base64 in JSON so it survives the body conversion HTTP call.

nodes.name: Build Body Files
nodes.notes: Body PDF inputs: content + Gotenberg-recognised header.html (running hero banner) + footer.html (page number).

nodes.name: Gotenberg Body
nodes.notes: Body HTML -> PDF with running hero banner header on every page + page-number footer.

nodes.name: Assemble PDFs
nodes.notes: Place cover + body PDFs on one item with order-prefixed filenames for the merge.

nodes.name: Gotenberg Merge
nodes.notes: Merge clean cover (page 1) in front of the banner+numbered body (pages 2+) into the final brief.

nodes.name: Write PDF
nodes.notes: Saves the branded PDF (binary). Permissions on reports/ allow the container uid to write.

nodes.name: Fetch Market Prices
nodes.notes: Live oil & gas benchmark prices (Yahoo v8 chart, no API key). Failure is non-fatal: financial cards fall back to analyst/'Not specified'.
